message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: My feature: add dry_run option to Image.save(). I need to edit src/PIL/Image.py (save method) and Tests/test_image.py. Please tell me which files you need so we can keep edits disjoint - if you also need Image.py, let's coordinate on non-overlapping regions.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Adding backup=True/backup_suffix param to Image.save(). Need to touch Image.py save() and its tests. Please propose your files so we can confirm disjointness.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: We both need the same 2 files since both features add a param to save(). True disjoint file split isn't possible. Proposal: serialize instead of splitting by file. I'll go first - my change is small/isolated (add backup/backup_suffix kwargs at top of save(), before format resolution, plus one test). I'll send DONE with a summary of exact lines changed once finished. Then you rebase your dry_run param on top of my version, adding your kwarg alongside mine (not replacing my lines). If you'd rather go first, reply REVISE and I'll wait - just let's not edit simultaneously to avoid clobbering.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: We both need Image.py save() + test_image.py, so file-level disjointness isn't possible - true overlap in the same function. Proposing a sequential handoff instead: I'll implement dry_run first (small, localized - I only touch the block around 'if open_fp:' to skip real disk writes, and add one new test function). I'll send DONE when finished. Please wait for my DONE, then re-read Image.py/test_image.py fresh off disk before adding your backup/backup_suffix logic, and add your code without touching/reverting my dry_run lines. If that works for you, ACCEPT; otherwise propose swapping the order instead.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed - you go first with dry_run. I will wait for your DONE message, then re-read both files fresh off disk before adding my backup/backup_suffix kwargs and test, without touching or reverting your dry_run lines.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Agreed - you go first with backup/backup_suffix. I'll wait for your DONE message before touching Image.py/test_image.py, then rebase my dry_run addition on top of your changes without reverting your lines.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: I see a mismatch: your last REVISE said you'd go first with dry_run, but your ACCEPT says I should go first with backup. To avoid us both editing simultaneously, let's lock this in explicitly: I WILL GO FIRST now. I will implement backup/backup_suffix in src/PIL/Image.py (small addition near the top of save(), before format resolution) and one test in Tests/test_image.py, then send DONE with the exact lines/diff summary. Please do not edit either file until you see my DONE. Reply ACCEPT to confirm you will wait, and I'll start immediately.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Confirmed - you go first with backup/backup_suffix. I will not touch Image.py or test_image.py until I see your DONE message. Go ahead.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished. Changes: (1) added 'import shutil' near top imports. (2) Image.save() signature changed to: def save(self, fp, format=None, *, backup: bool = False, backup_suffix: str = '.bak', **params) -> None: — note I made these KEYWORD-ONLY (after a bare '*'), so your dry_run param should also be added as keyword-only after the '*' to avoid positional-arg confusion. (3) Added docstring lines for :param backup: and :param backup_suffix:. (4) In the body, inside 'if open_fp:' block, right after 'created = not os.path.exists(filename)' and BEFORE the 'if params.get("append", False):' line, I added a block that copies the existing file to a backup path via shutil.copy2 when backup=True and the file already existed. Nothing else in save() was touched - the rest of the function (save_handler call, exception handling, fp.close()) is unchanged. In Tests/test_image.py I added 'import' is already present (shutil, Path already imported at top), and added 3 new test methods (test_save_backup, test_save_backup_custom_suffix, test_save_no_backup_by_default) right before 'def test_dump' inside TestImage class (right after test_readonly_save). All 195 existing tests + my 3 new ones pass. Go ahead and add your dry_run logic - just insert it without touching my added lines. Let me know if you want the exact diff.

type: REVISE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
your_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Heads up: on my end, git status/diff shows a clean working tree and I don't see 'shutil', 'backup', or a modified save() signature anywhere in src/PIL/Image.py or Tests/test_image.py - your described changes aren't visible in my copy of the repo. We may be in separate sandboxes. I'll proceed to implement dry_run on my current (unmodified) copy of save(). If our final patches need to be merged later, my diff will be additive/localized to the save() body and won't assume your backup code is present. Will send DONE when finished.

type: DONE
my_files: src/PIL/Image.py,Tests/test_image.py
note: Finished dry_run implementation. In save(): added 'dry_run = params.pop("dry_run", False)' right after the existing 'save_all = params.pop(...)' line (before self.encoderinfo assignment). In the 'created = False' block, changed 'if open_fp:' to 'if dry_run: fp = io.BytesIO() elif open_fp: ...' (unchanged branch otherwise), and changed the two 'if open_fp:' close-fp guards (in the except block and after the try/finally) to 'if open_fp and not dry_run:'. Added a :param dry_run: docstring line. No signature change (dry_run flows through **params like save_all), so it's NOT keyword-only-after-* like your backup params - just a regular **params key. Added 4 tests in TestImage right after test_readonly_save (test_save_dry_run, test_save_dry_run_file_object, test_save_dry_run_raises_error, test_save_dry_run_does_not_overwrite_existing_file), before test_dump. All 196 tests in Tests/test_image.py pass. On my end the working tree still only shows my 2-file diff (49 lines) - I still don't see any backup/shutil changes, so please double check your sandbox actually persisted your edits before you finalize. Go ahead with your changes now - I'm done touching these files.
